title: Fn overloading: entity overload ban
analysis: Spec §4.2.1 explicitly forbids entity definition overloading: two entity definitions (structures, occurrences, constraints, fields) sharing the same name are a compile error, regardless of type parameters. Currently, only duplicate field names are detected (pre-pass lines 1858-1877 in lib.rs via seen_field_names HashMap). Structures, occurrences, and constraints have NO duplicate name detection — duplicates silently overwrite in the template registry (last-definition-wins via HashMap::collect). The fix introduces a unified seen_entity_names tracker in the consolidated pre-pass that checks ALL entity definition types against a single namespace. Duplicates emit a two-label diagnostic (matching the existing field pattern) and are skipped from compilation. The existing seen_field_names HashMap is replaced by the unified tracker. Constraints are tracked even though their compilation is not yet implemented, to reserve names in the entity namespace for future use. The implementation follows the existing duplicate-detection pattern: pre-pass tracking with HashMap<String, (SourceSpan, entity_kind)>, diagnostic with two labels pointing to both definitions, and skip-on-duplicate to prevent cascading errors.

steps.description: Create entity_overload_tests.rs with test: two structures with the same name (e.g., `structure Bracket { ... }` twice) produce a 'duplicate entity definition' error diagnostic. Verify the diagnostic contains both labels and only 1 template is compiled.

In compile() pre-pass (~line 1841), add `seen_entity_names: HashMap<String, (SourceSpan, &'static str)>` and `duplicate_entity_names: HashSet<String>`. Add a Structure arm to the pre-pass match that checks against seen_entity_names, emits a two-label diagnostic on collision (with entity kind in labels), and records the name in duplicate_entity_names. In pass 2 (~line 1922), wrap the Structure arm in a `if !duplicate_entity_names.contains(name)` guard to skip duplicate compilation.
Write test: two occurrences with the same name produce a 'duplicate entity definition' error. Verify only 1 template is compiled.
Add Occurrence arm to pre-pass entity name tracking (same pattern as Structure). In pass 2 (~line 1951), add duplicate_entity_names guard for occurrence compilation.

Write test: a structure and an occurrence with the same name produce a 'duplicate entity definition' error. Diagnostic labels should identify the entity kinds (e.g., 'occurrence defined here' / 'first defined as structure here'). Verify only 1 template is compiled.

Should already pass with the unified tracker from steps 2+4. If cross-entity collision is not detected, verify the seen_entity_names lookup uses the same key for both entity kinds. This step is a verification/fix gate.
Write test: a field def and a structure with the same name produce a 'duplicate entity definition' error. The first-declared entity wins regardless of kind.

Refactor the existing field duplicate check (lines 1858-1877) to use the unified seen_entity_names tracker instead of the separate seen_field_names HashMap. Remove seen_field_names. The Field arm now inserts into seen_entity_names with kind='field' and checks against it, emitting 'duplicate entity definition' with entity-kind-aware labels on collision. Existing field-vs-field duplicate test must still pass.
Write test: a constraint def and a structure with the same name produce a 'duplicate entity definition' error.
Add Constraint arm to pre-pass entity name tracking. Constraints are not compiled yet (line 1962-1963 is a no-op) but their names are now reserved in the entity namespace to prevent collisions.

Write test: two structures with the same name but different type parameters (e.g., `structure Box<T> { ... }` and `structure Box<T, U> { ... }`) still produce a 'duplicate entity definition' error. Confirms name comparison ignores type params per spec §4.2.1.

Write regression/sanity test: unique entity names across all types (structure A, occurrence B, field C, constraint D) compile without entity-duplicate errors. Ensures the check does not false-positive on distinct names.

design_decisions.decision: Unified entity namespace across all four entity types (structure, occurrence, constraint, field)
design_decisions.rationale: Spec §4.2.1 explicitly lists all four as sharing the entity namespace: 'Two entity definitions (structures, occurrences, constraints, fields) with the same name... are an error.' A structure named Foo and a field named Foo must collide.

design_decisions.decision: Detection in the consolidated pre-pass rather than post-compilation
design_decisions.rationale: The pre-pass already detects duplicate field names and is the earliest point where all declarations are visited. Detecting in pre-pass enables skipping compilation of duplicates, avoiding cascading errors from invalid state. Matches existing field duplicate detection pattern.

design_decisions.decision: Replace seen_field_names with unified seen_entity_names tracker
design_decisions.rationale: Having two separate trackers (one for fields, one for entities) would miss cross-type collisions (field vs structure). A single HashMap<String, (SourceSpan, &str)> keyed by name, storing the first span and entity kind, handles all cases uniformly.

design_decisions.decision: Error message format: 'duplicate entity definition <name>' with entity-kind-aware labels
design_decisions.rationale: The existing field diagnostic says 'duplicate field name'. The unified message uses 'duplicate entity definition' to cover cross-type collisions, with labels like 'occurrence defined here' and 'first defined as structure here' to clarify which entity kinds are involved.

design_decisions.decision: Track constraint names even though constraint compilation is not yet implemented
design_decisions.rationale: The spec includes constraints in the entity namespace. Reserving names now prevents future breakage when constraint compilation is added. The pre-pass tracking cost is negligible.

design_decisions.decision: Name-only comparison (ignore type parameters) for entity duplicate detection
design_decisions.rationale: Per spec §4.2.1 and the task description: 'regardless of type parameters'. Entity overloading is forbidden precisely because type-param-based dispatch is ambiguous under partial determinacy. This is simpler than function signature comparison which includes param types.

reuse.what: Field duplicate detection pattern (two-label diagnostic, skip on duplicate)
reuse.how: Generalize the existing seen_field_names HashMap to a unified seen_entity_names tracker. Reuse the two-label Diagnostic::error pattern with DiagnosticLabel::new for both 'defined here' and 'first defined here' locations.

Add new match arms for Structure, Occurrence, and Constraint in the existing pre-pass match dispatch. The loop already has a wildcard arm for these types (line 1879-1880) that gets replaced with explicit handling.
Use the same parse → compile → filter diagnostics by Severity::Error → assert pattern. Use reify_syntax::parse() with ModulePath::single() and reify_compiler::compile().

reuse.where: Analogous to field skip at line 1874 (field_refs not pushed on duplicate)
reuse.how: For structures/occurrences, the duplicate skip must happen in pass 2 (lines 1920-1969) since their compilation occurs there rather than in the pre-pass. Use a HashSet<String> populated in pre-pass, checked with .contains() before compile_entity() calls.
